questions.text: Which electrosurgical mode is designed to rapidly heat tissue, causing intracellular water to vaporize and resulting in cellular rupture?
questions.explanation: Cutting mode uses low-voltage, continuous waveforms to quickly heat tissue and create a vapor pocket that bursts cells.

questions.text: Applying monopolar energy directly to the tip of a ligated structure, such as the appendix stump, poses the highest risk of thermal injury in which area?

answerOptions.text: B) The tissue immediately proximal to the ligature

questions.explanation: Current density is highest just before the ligature where the diameter narrows, increasing the risk of delayed thermal injury.

questions.text: Which type of trocar minimizes the risk of capacitive coupling during monopolar electrosurgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Trocars with hybrid plastic-metal construction
C) Trocars with removable inner sheaths

questions.explanation: Hybrid trocars can act as capacitors and store energy; single-material designs avoid this issue.

questions.text: To reduce the risk of inadvertent tissue injury from ultrasonic shears, which strategy is most appropriate?

answerOptions.text: A) Activate the blade in rapid bursts
B) Keep the passive jaw below the target tissue
C) Ensure blade orientation and full visualization during use
D) Rely on tactile feedback to guide cutting

questions.explanation: The active blade is unshielded; keeping it in view reduces risk of unintended thermal contact.

questions.text: Which factor increases current density during monopolar electrosurgery?
questions.explanation: Concentrating current into a smaller area increases current density and accelerates tissue heating.

questions.text: Which waveform is commonly used for superficial coagulation with wide heat dispersion but minimal tissue penetration?
questions.explanation: Coagulation mode uses high-voltage, pulsed currents for surface-level tissue desiccation.

questions.text: Which of the following actions would increase the risk of stray current injury during monopolar laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Using bipolar energy for dissection
C) Activating monopolar energy near another instrument
D) Choosing a pure cut waveform

questions.explanation: Energy can unintentionally transfer to adjacent instruments and tissues through direct or capacitive coupling.

questions.text: What cellular process contributes most to vessel sealing during coagulation?
questions.explanation: Heat causes proteins to denature and tissues to dehydrate, forming a fibrous coagulum for hemostasis.

questions.text: Which scenario increases the likelihood of capacitive coupling?

answerOptions.text: A) Metal port in constant contact with tissue
B) Insulated active electrode inside a metal cannula
C) Activation of ultrasonic energy outside the field of view
D) Bipolar use with exposed metal jaw

questions.explanation: This forms a capacitor system where current can transfer from the active to passive conductor through the insulation.

questions.text: A monopolar active electrode accidentally touches a grasper holding bowel. What complication is most likely?

answerOptions.text: D) Capacitive discharge to the port

questions.explanation: This is a form of unintended direct coupling that can result in significant tissue burns.

questions.text: Why is bipolar energy generally safer than monopolar in laparoscopic procedures?

answerOptions.text: B) Current is confined between two electrodes in the same instrument
C) It always provides better visualization
D) No heating of tissue occurs

questions.explanation: Bipolar systems limit energy delivery to a small volume of tissue, avoiding the dispersive circuit.

questions.text: What is the sealing capacity of most advanced bipolar vessel sealing systems?
questions.explanation: Most modern bipolar sealing devices can reliably seal vessels up to 7 mm in diameter.

questions.text: Which technology allows bipolar vessel sealing devices to modulate energy based on real-time tissue feedback?
questions.explanation: These sensors adjust energy delivery based on the tissue's resistance, improving safety and seal quality.

questions.text: In ultrasonic energy devices, what component holds tissue in place while the vibrating blade seals and cuts it?
questions.explanation: The passive jaw traps tissue against the active blade, allowing for focused energy transfer.

questions.text: Which outcome is associated with using higher power settings on ultrasonic instruments?

answerOptions.text: B) Increased risk of capacitive coupling

questions.explanation: High power settings increase vibration amplitude, enhancing the cutting speed but reducing coagulation efficiency.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes insulation failure in laparoscopic instruments?

answerOptions.text: A) Electrical arcing due to power surges
B) A break in the instrument's outer coating that allows unintended energy discharge
C) Leakage of fluid from within the shaft
D) Inadequate sterilization of electrosurgical tools

questions.explanation: Insulation failure allows monopolar current to escape the shaft and potentially burn adjacent tissue, often outside the field of view.

questions.text: Which is the safest location for placing the dispersive (grounding) electrode?

answerOptions.text: A) Over a scarred area for secure adhesion
B) On the bony prominence of the hip
C) On dry, hairy skin on the back
D) On a well-vascularized, flat muscle surface

questions.explanation: Full contact with vascularized muscle ensures proper energy dispersion and reduces the risk of burns.

questions.text: Which of the following factors contributes to dispersive electrode burns?

answerOptions.text: C) Poor pad contact due to hair or scars

questions.explanation: Poor contact increases current concentration, which can cause thermal injury at the pad site.

questions.text: Which of the following is an appropriate clinical scenario for using bipolar vessel sealing?

answerOptions.text: A) Cutting through bowel with mesentery
C) Sealing the infundibulopelvic ligament during salpingo-oophorectomy
D) Opening a cyst with monopolar energy

questions.explanation: Bipolar sealing is effective for controlling vascular pedicles such as the infundibulopelvic ligament in adnexal surgery.

questions.text: Which energy source typically produces the least lateral thermal spread?
questions.explanation: Ultrasonic shears rely on mechanical vibration with minimal heat transfer to surrounding tissues.

questions.text: Why is it important to use a torque wrench when attaching the ultrasonic transducer?

answerOptions.text: B) It ensures a secure sterile barrier
C) It guarantees optimal energy transfer and function

questions.explanation: Proper torque ensures the transducer and handpiece are coupled effectively for consistent ultrasonic vibration.
